answer:
The MongoDB Atlas Administration API supports two primary authentication methods: OAuth2-based service accounts and HTTP Digest-based API keys [1][2]. Service accounts are the recommended method due to their use of short-lived access tokens, while API keys are considered a legacy option [1][3]. To use a service account, exchange your client ID and secret for a Bearer token via the OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow at the following endpoint [1][3]:

```
POST https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/oauth/token
```

When using legacy API keys, you must provide your public and private keys using HTTP Digest authentication [2][4]. It is recommended to implement IP access lists and rotate keys regularly when using this method [2].
